title: REGIONAL CODE OF HYGIENIC PRACTICE FOR STREET- VENDED FOODS

sections.section: REGIONAL CODE OF HYGIENIC PRACTICE FOR STREET- VENDED FOODS
sections.summary: This is the title of the 'REGIONAL CODE OF HYGIENIC PRACTICE FOR STREET-VENDED FOODS' and serves as the document heading.
Document reference indicating this Regional Code (CXC 76R-2017) was adopted in Asia in 2017.

Defines 'street-vended foods' as foods/beverages prepared and/or sold by vendors in streets/public places for immediate or later consumption without further processing, notes urbanization-driven expansion and highlights street foods' role in affordability, livelihoods and preservation of local culinary traditions.
States the Code's objective to address essential hygienic conditions to be considered when establishing street food control measures.
Defines the Code's scope as setting general hygienic practices required to make street foods safe and notes it is intended to be useful for vendors/food handlers, consumers and relevant authorities.
States that the Code is intended for use by Asian countries to ensure the safety and hygiene of street-vended foods.

Provides definitions of terms used in the Code relevant to street-vended foods—e.g., equipment, clean/clean water, contaminant, crockery/cutlery, cross-contamination, customer/consumer, disposable articles, food contact surfaces, food handler, food hygiene/safety, microorganisms, perishable food, pests, potable/drinking water (WHO), raw food, ready-to-eat, sealed containers, street foods/centres/stalls/vendors, and waste water—with references to related Codex/WHO documents.
Defines 'street foods' as foods and beverages prepared and/or sold by vendors in streets and other public places for immediate or later consumption.
Defines 'Street Food Centre' as a public place or establishment designated by the relevant authority for preparation, display and sale of street foods by multiple vendors.
Defines 'Street Food Stall' as any place (including carts, tables, benches, baskets, vehicles, or similar structures) where street food is prepared, displayed, served or sold to the public.
Defines 'Street food vendor' as an individual selling street foods in stalls, kiosks, carts or centres.
Defines 'Waste Water' as sullage water generated by vendors' activities.
Section heading only (GENERAL REQUIREMENTS) and contains no substantive provisions in this excerpt.

Advises that appropriate rules and regulations should be drafted to enable effective implementation and control of the street foods sector, either as separate rules or incorporated into existing food regulations.

sections.section: 3.2 Registration / Licensing of Street Food Vendors
sections.summary: Where national legislation requires, street food vendors must have registration/licensing from the relevant authority and display identification on their cart/kiosk; the authority should issue or renew registration for vendors who comply with the Code's hygienic practice requirements.

sections.section: SECTION 4 - STAKEHOLDERS IN STREET FOOD VENDING
sections.summary: Section heading noting that it addresses the roles and responsibilities of three key stakeholders in street food vending.

Specifies vendor obligations for personal hygiene (clean clothing, hair nets, disposable gloves when needed, short clean nails, no jewellery, covered wounds), hygienic behaviour (no eating/chewing/smoking/handling phones or currency while preparing food, no sneezing/coughing over food, and mandatory handwashing at key times), and not working when showing symptoms such as jaundice, diarrhoea or infected skin lesions; these are to be ensured through awareness, motivation and training.
Advises consumers to avoid eating/chewing/smoking/spitting or touching near displayed food, not to litter or cough/sneeze near food, and to dispose of leftovers in waste bins.

Prescribes handling and storage controls: cover and protect food from dust, use clean crockery/utensils, use single-use disposables once, keep ready-to-eat foods covered/displayed in cabinets, dispose of leftovers/unsold perishables, separate raw and cooked foods, store seasonings appropriately, serve in individual containers, refrigerate cooked/perishable foods promptly (preferably below 5°C), store fuels/chemicals away from food zones, ensure hygienic transport within shelf life limits, and wash hands after handling non-food items.

Requires basic food hygiene training for every street food vendor, helper and food handler provided by relevant authorities or approved institutions using concise, pictorial, local-language IEC materials (waterproof if needed) and calls for consumer awareness of their responsibilities.
